Title: dmac_reg_wr_rd_test
Description: Verifies 92 DMAC registers listed in test_define.c: first checks reset default values (read vs default_value_array under read_mask), then performs masked write-read verification across 6 patterns for writable/readable addresses; aggregates failures in def_fail_cnt and wr_fail_cnt; calls finish(0) only when both counters remain zero.
Steps:
Entry: test_case() begins.
Call chk_rst_val().
Function chk_rst_val(): for i=0..CNT-1: addr = addr_array[i]. If read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: optionally printf and continue. Else: data_rd = read_reg(addr). If data_rd == default_value_array[i]: optionally printf PASS; else: def_fail_cnt++ and printf failure with addr/expected/read. Return to caller.
Back in test_case(): call chk_rd_wr().
Function chk_rd_wr(): define chk_val[6] = {0xffffffff, 0xaaaaaaaa, 0x55555555, 0x00000000, 0xA5A5A5A5, 0xffff0000}. For each j=0..5: set data_wr = chk_val[j].
Write phase for pattern j: for i=0..CNT-1: addr = addr_array[i]. If skip_array[i] == 1: optionally printf and continue. If write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: optionally printf and continue. Else: write_reg(addr, data_wr) and optionally print address/data.
Read/verify phase for pattern j: for i=0..CNT-1: addr = addr_array[i]. If skip_array[i] == 1: continue (with optional print). If write_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: continue (with optional print). If read_mask_array[i] == 0x00000000: continue (with optional print). Else: data_rd = read_reg(addr); wr_n = (write_mask_array[i] ^ 0xffffffff); exp_val = ((data_wr & read_mask_array[i] & write_mask_array[i]) | (wr_n & read_mask_array[i] & default_value_array[i])); If data_rd == exp_val: optionally printf PASS; else: wr_fail_cnt++ and printf failure with addr/exp/read.
Return to caller after all patterns complete.
Back in test_case(): if (def_fail_cnt > 0 || wr_fail_cnt > 0) finish(1); else finish(0).
Expected Result: Pass only when def_fail_cnt == 0 and wr_fail_cnt == 0 causing finish(0); any mismatch increments a counter and triggers finish(1).
